rawText:
Most people do not think about how a product reaches a store shelf until the day they walk in and find it missing.

A delivery chain — also called a supply chain — is the series of steps that moves goods from the place where they are made to the place where they are sold.

Each step depends on the one before it, which means that a delay at any single point can ripple through the entire system.

Consider a simple example: a factory in one country produces electronic parts that are shipped by sea to an assembly plant in another country.

If a storm closes the port for three days, the container ships must wait, and every factory that needs those parts falls behind schedule.

The assembly plant cannot finish its products on time, so the warehouse that was expecting a shipment next week receives nothing.

Retailers who depend on that warehouse then face empty shelves, and customers are told that the item is out of stock with no clear date for restocking.

What began as a weather event at a single port has now affected manufacturers, shippers, warehouses, stores, and millions of consumers.

During the global disruptions of the early 2020s, the world saw this pattern play out on a massive scale.

Shipping containers piled up at ports, truck drivers were in short supply, and everyday items like paper towels and computer chips became difficult to find.

Companies responded by rethinking their strategies: some moved production closer to their main markets, while others began keeping larger inventories as a safety buffer.

Governments also stepped in, investing in port upgrades and encouraging the development of alternative transportation routes.

These changes have made supply chains somewhat more resilient, but no system that spans the entire globe can be made fully immune to disruption.

The lesson is clear — the modern economy is a connected web, and what happens at one node is eventually felt everywhere else.

overview.title: What Happens When a Delivery Chain Slows Down

sentences.text: When you go to a store and something is missing, it is because the delivery got delayed somewhere.
sentences.translation: 가게에 갔는데 물건이 없으면, 어딘가에서 배달이 늦어졌기 때문이에요.

sentences.text: Products travel through many steps before they reach the shelf where you can buy them.
sentences.translation: 물건은 우리가 살 수 있는 선반에 오기까지 아주 많은 단계를 거쳐요.

sentences.text: If one step is late, all the other steps after it become late too.
sentences.translation: 한 단계가 늦어지면, 그 뒤의 모든 단계도 다 늦어져요.

sentences.text: A few years ago, this happened all over the world and many things were hard to buy.
sentences.translation: 몇 년 전에 전 세계에서 이런 일이 생겨서, 많은 물건을 사기 어려웠어요.

sentences.text: Companies and governments tried to fix the problem so it would not happen again.
sentences.translation: 회사와 정부가 다시는 이런 일이 안 생기도록 문제를 고치려고 했어요.

sentences.text: But because the world is so connected, it is impossible to stop every delay completely.
sentences.translation: 하지만 세상이 너무 연결되어 있어서, 모든 지연을 완전히 막는 건 불가능해요.

sentences.text: The article explains how supply chains work and why a disruption at one point affects everything downstream.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 공급망이 어떻게 작동하는지, 한 지점의 차질이 왜 하류 전체에 영향을 미치는지 설명해요.

sentences.text: A supply chain is the sequence of steps that moves goods from manufacturers to consumers.
sentences.translation: 공급망은 제조업체에서 소비자까지 물건을 이동시키는 일련의 단계예요.

sentences.text: The writer uses a storm-at-a-port scenario to show how one delay cascades through factories, warehouses, and stores.
sentences.translation: 글쓴이는 항구의 폭풍 시나리오를 들어, 하나의 지연이 공장·창고·매장으로 어떻게 연쇄적으로 퍼지는지 보여줘요.

sentences.text: The early 2020s serve as a real-world case study of massive supply-chain failure.
sentences.translation: 2020년대 초반은 대규모 공급망 붕괴의 실제 사례로 제시돼요.

sentences.text: In response, companies diversified production locations and governments invested in infrastructure.
sentences.translation: 이에 대응해 기업은 생산지를 다변화하고, 정부는 인프라에 투자했어요.

sentences.text: The article concludes that global interconnection makes total immunity to disruption impossible.
sentences.translation: 글은 글로벌 상호 연결이 차질에 대한 완전한 면역을 불가능하게 만든다고 결론 지어요.

sentences.text: This expository piece uses a cause-and-effect chain structure to demystify supply-chain logistics for a general audience.
sentences.translation: 이 설명문은 인과 연쇄 구조를 사용해 일반 독자에게 공급망 물류를 알기 쉽게 풀어줘요.

sentences.text: The opening relies on a relatable consumer experience — an empty shelf — to hook readers before introducing technical concepts.
sentences.translation: 도입부는 빈 선반이라는 공감 가능한 소비자 경험으로 독자를 끌어들인 후 기술적 개념을 소개해요.

sentences.text: Passive-voice constructions ("are shipped," "are told," "is eventually felt") foreground the process over individual actors, reinforcing the systems-thinking lens.
sentences.translation: 수동태 구문이 개별 행위자보다 과정을 부각시켜 시스템 사고 관점을 강화해요.

sentences.text: The hypothetical port-storm scenario functions as an extended analogy, walking readers step-by-step through cascading failure.
sentences.translation: 가상의 항구 폭풍 시나리오가 확장된 비유로 기능하며, 독자를 연쇄 실패의 단계별 과정으로 안내해요.

sentences.text: The pivot from historical example to forward-looking solutions mirrors the problem-solution essay pattern common in academic writing.
sentences.translation: 역사적 사례에서 미래 지향적 해결책으로의 전환은 학술 글쓰기에서 흔한 문제-해결 에세이 패턴을 반영해요.

sentences.text: The closing metaphor — "a connected web" — encapsulates the thesis and gives readers a memorable mental model for global interdependence.
sentences.translation: 마무리 은유인 "연결된 거미줄"이 논지를 응축하고, 글로벌 상호의존에 대한 기억에 남는 심상을 독자에게 선사해요.

sentences.original: Most people do not think about how a product reaches a store shelf until the day they walk in and find it missing.
sentences.translation: 대부분의 사람들은 어느 날 가게에 들어가서 물건이 없는 것을 발견할 때까지, 제품이 어떻게 매장 선반에 도달하는지 생각하지 않는다.

grammarElements.text: how a product reaches a store shelf
grammarElements.label: 간접의문문 (how)

grammarElements.text: until the day they walk in and find it missing
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절 (until)

vocabulary.example: This product is made in Korea.
The item was not on the shelf.
Several items were missing from the display.

expressions.usage: 평소 인식하지 못하는 것을 지적할 때 쓰는 표현
예상치 못한 상황을 마주하는 순간을 묘사할 때 쓴다

patterns.explanation: 사람들이 평소 인식하지 못하다가 특정 사건을 계기로 깨닫게 되는 상황을 도입하는 패턴. 글의 도입부에서 독자의 관심을 끌 때 효과적이다.
examples: Most drivers do not think about road maintenance until the day they hit a pothole.
People do not think about clean water until the day they turn on the tap and nothing comes out.

drillQuestions.question: Most people do not think about how a product _____ a store shelf.
They do not notice _____ they find the item missing.
"Find it missing" means to discover something is _____.
"How a product reaches a store shelf" is a(n) _____ question clause.

sentences.notes: 도입부로, 독자의 일상 경험에서 출발하여 주제에 대한 관심을 유도한다. "do not think about ~ until ~" 구조로 무관심에서 인식으로의 전환점을 설정하며, 간접의문문(how a product reaches)이 글의 탐구 방향을 예고한다.

koreanAlignment.en: how a product reaches a store shelf
until the day they walk in and find it missing
koreanAlignment.ko: 어느 날 가게에 들어가서 물건이 없는 것을 발견할 때까지

pronunciationNotes: product: /ˈprɑːdʌkt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "프라덕트"에 가깝다. 끝의 -ct를 명확히 발음한다.
shelf: /ʃelf/ — sh를 부드럽게 "쉬"로 시작. 복수형은 shelves /ʃelvz/로 f가 v로 바뀐다.

sentences.original: A delivery chain — also called a supply chain — is the series of steps that moves goods from the place where they are made to the place where they are sold.
sentences.translation: 배달 체인 — 공급망이라고도 불리는 — 은 물건을 만들어지는 곳에서 판매되는 곳까지 이동시키는 일련의 단계이다.

grammarElements.text: that moves goods from the place where they are made to the place where they are sold
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (that) + 장소 관계절 (where) × 2

vocabulary.example: The delivery chain was disrupted by the storm.
Global supply chains are complex systems.
The goods were shipped overseas.

expressions.usage: 동의어나 별칭을 소개할 때 쓰는 삽입 표현
순차적인 과정을 설명할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.explanation: 전문 용어를 소개하며 대시(—)로 별칭을 삽입하고, 정의를 제시하는 패턴. 설명문에서 핵심 개념을 도입할 때 쓴다.
examples: A habitat — also called a living environment — is the natural home of a plant or animal.
An algorithm — also called a set of instructions — is a step-by-step procedure for solving a problem.

drillQuestions.question: A delivery chain is also called a _____ chain.
A supply chain moves _____ from where they are made to where they are sold.
"The place where they are made" uses a _____ clause.
"Also called" is used to introduce a _____.

sentences.notes: 핵심 용어(delivery chain / supply chain)를 정의하는 문장. 대시(—)로 별칭을 삽입하고, 관계절(that moves ~)과 장소 관계절(where they are made / where they are sold)로 정의를 구체화한다. 수동태(are made, are sold)로 과정 중심의 설명을 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: from the place where they are made
to the place where they are sold

pronunciationNotes: chain: /tʃeɪn/ — ch를 "체"로 시작. "체인"에 가깝다. "채인"이 아니다.
supply: /səˈplaɪ/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "서플라이"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Each step depends on the one before it, which means that a delay at any single point can ripple through the entire system.
sentences.translation: 각 단계는 그 앞의 단계에 의존하며, 이는 어느 한 지점에서의 지연이 전체 시스템에 파급될 수 있다는 것을 뜻한다.

grammarElements.text: which means that a delay at any single point can ripple through the entire system
grammarElements.label: 계속적 관계절 (which means that ~)

vocabulary.example: The success of the project depends on teamwork.
A three-day delay caused major problems.
The effects rippled through the economy.

expressions.usage: 앞 내용의 함의나 결과를 자연스럽게 연결할 때 쓰는 계속적 관계절 표현
영향이 연쇄적으로 확산되는 모습을 물결 비유로 표현한다

patterns.pattern: Each [noun] depends on the one before it, which means that [consequence]
patterns.explanation: 순차적 의존 관계를 설명한 뒤, which means that으로 그 결과를 도출하는 패턴. 시스템의 취약성을 설명할 때 효과적이다.
examples: Each layer of the cake depends on the one below it, which means that a weak base can ruin the whole structure.
Each link in the process depends on the one before it, which means that a mistake early on can multiply.

drillQuestions.question: Each step _____ on the one before it.
A delay at one point can _____ through the entire system.
"The one before it" refers to the _____ step.
"Any single point" means _____ point in the chain.

sentences.notes: 공급망의 핵심 원리인 순차적 의존성을 설명하는 문장. "ripple through"라는 물결 은유가 연쇄 효과를 생동감 있게 전달한다. "any single point"로 시스템의 취약성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: a delay at any single point
can ripple through the entire system
koreanAlignment.ko: 전체 시스템에 파급될 수 있다는 것

pronunciationNotes: ripple: /ˈrɪpl/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "리플"에 가깝다. p 뒤의 l을 가볍게 발음한다.
entire: /ɪnˈtaɪər/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인타이어"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Consider a simple example: a factory in one country produces electronic parts that are shipped by sea to an assembly plant in another country.
sentences.translation: 간단한 예를 들어 보자: 한 나라의 공장이 전자 부품을 생산하고, 그 부품은 배로 다른 나라의 조립 공장으로 운송된다.

grammarElements.text: that are shipped by sea to an assembly plant in another country
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (that + 수동태)

vocabulary.example: The factory makes electronic parts for phones.
The goods are shipped by sea.
The assembly plant is located in Mexico.

expressions.usage: 독자에게 구체적 사례를 제시하며 이해를 돕는 도입 표현
해상 운송 방식을 나타내는 표현

patterns.explanation: 명령형으로 예시를 도입하고, 콜론 뒤에 구체적인 시나리오를 수동태 관계절로 전개하는 패턴.
examples: Consider a common case: a farmer grows tomatoes that are trucked to a distribution center in the city.
Consider a typical scenario: a designer creates plans that are sent by email to a builder overseas.

drillQuestions.question: _____ a simple example of how supply chains work.
Electronic parts are shipped _____ sea to another country.
"Assembly plant" is where products are _____.
"That are shipped" is a _____ voice construction.

sentences.notes: 명령형(Consider)으로 독자를 직접 참여시키며 구체적 시나리오를 시작한다. 콜론(:) 뒤에 예시가 이어지고, 수동태 관계절(that are shipped)로 과정 중심의 설명을 한다. 이후 문장들의 연쇄 효과 서사를 위한 출발점이다.

koreanAlignment.en: to an assembly plant in another country

pronunciationNotes: electronic: /ˌɪlekˈtrɑːnɪk/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "일렉트로닉"에 가깝다.
assembly: /əˈsembli/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어셈블리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: If a storm closes the port for three days, the container ships must wait, and every factory that needs those parts falls behind schedule.
sentences.translation: 만약 폭풍이 항구를 3일간 폐쇄하면, 컨테이너선은 기다려야 하고, 그 부품이 필요한 모든 공장은 일정에 뒤처지게 된다.

grammarElements.text: If a storm closes the port for three days
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (If)

grammarElements.text: every factory that needs those parts
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The port was closed due to the storm.
Container ships carry goods across oceans.
The project fell behind schedule.

expressions.usage: 계획된 시간보다 늦어지는 상황을 나타내는 관용 표현
must로 선택의 여지가 없는 상황을 표현한다

patterns.explanation: 가정적 사건을 If절로 제시하고, 그에 따른 연쇄적 결과 두 가지를 병렬로 나열하는 패턴.
examples: If a fire shuts down the highway for two hours, commuters must find detours, and deliveries across the region fall behind schedule.
If heavy rain floods the railway for a week, freight trains must reroute, and every supplier waiting for materials faces delays.

drillQuestions.question: If a storm closes the port, the container ships _____ wait.
Every factory that needs those parts falls _____ schedule.
"If a storm closes the port" is a _____ clause.
"Every factory that needs those parts" contains a _____ clause.

sentences.notes: If절로 가상 시나리오의 첫 번째 원인(폭풍)을 설정하고, 두 가지 즉각적 결과를 제시한다. "every factory that needs those parts"라는 관계절이 영향의 광범위함을 강조한다. 연쇄 효과 서사의 첫 고리이다.

koreanAlignment.en: If a storm closes the port for three days
and every factory that needs those parts

pronunciationNotes: container: /kənˈteɪnər/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨테이너"에 가깝다.
schedule: /ˈskedʒuːl/ — 미국식은 "스케줄". 영국식은 /ˈʃedjuːl/ "셰듈"로 다르다.

sentences.original: The assembly plant cannot finish its products on time, so the warehouse that was expecting a shipment next week receives nothing.
sentences.translation: 조립 공장은 제때 제품을 완성할 수 없고, 그래서 다음 주 입고를 기대하던 창고는 아무것도 받지 못한다.

grammarElements.text: the warehouse that was expecting a shipment next week
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절 (과거진행)

vocabulary.example: The warehouse stores goods before they are shipped to stores.
The shipment was delayed by a week.
The store receives new stock every Monday.

expressions.usage: 예정된 시간에 맞춘다는 뜻의 일상 표현
완전한 부재를 강조하는 표현. nothing이 부정의 강도를 높인다

patterns.pattern: [Subject A] cannot [verb] on time, so [Subject B] that was expecting [noun] receives nothing
patterns.explanation: 한 단계의 실패가 다음 단계의 완전한 공백으로 이어지는 연쇄 효과를 보여주는 패턴.
examples: The bakery cannot bake enough bread on time, so the café that was expecting a delivery this morning receives nothing.
The supplier cannot ship materials on time, so the builder who was expecting them this week receives nothing.

drillQuestions.question: The assembly plant cannot finish its products _____ time.
The warehouse was _____ a shipment next week.
"So" in this sentence signals a _____.
The warehouse receives _____.

sentences.notes: 연쇄 효과의 2단계. "cannot finish on time"이 원인, "receives nothing"이 결과. 관계절(that was expecting)의 과거진행형이 기대와 현실의 괴리를 효과적으로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: that was expecting a shipment next week

pronunciationNotes: warehouse: /ˈwerhaʊs/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "웨어하우스"에 가깝다.
shipment: /ˈʃɪpmənt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "쉽먼트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Retailers who depend on that warehouse then face empty shelves, and customers are told that the item is out of stock with no clear date for restocking.
sentences.translation: 그 창고에 의존하는 소매업체는 빈 선반에 직면하게 되고, 고객들은 재입고 날짜도 불확실한 채 품절이라는 말을 듣게 된다.

grammarElements.text: Retailers who depend on that warehouse
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: that the item is out of stock with no clear date for restocking
grammarElements.label: that 명사절

vocabulary.example: Retailers struggled with empty shelves.
The product is currently out of stock.
There is no clear date for restocking.

expressions.usage: 재고 부족 상황을 시각적으로 묘사하는 표현
수동태로 정보를 전달받는 상황을 나타낸다. 고객이 통제할 수 없는 상황을 강조한다

patterns.pattern: [Group A] who depend on [source] then face [problem], and [Group B] are told that [bad news]
patterns.explanation: 의존 관계에 있는 집단이 문제에 직면하고, 최종 소비자가 부정적 소식을 전달받는 구조. 연쇄 효과의 마지막 단계를 보여준다.
examples: Restaurants who depend on that supplier then face menu shortages, and diners are told that their favorite dish is unavailable.
Pharmacies who depend on that distributor then face empty shelves, and patients are told that the medicine is on backorder.

drillQuestions.question: Retailers who _____ on that warehouse face empty shelves.
Customers are told the item is out of _____.
"Are told" is in the _____ voice.
"No clear date for restocking" means the _____ is uncertain.

sentences.notes: 연쇄 효과의 3단계이자 최종 소비자 단계. "face empty shelves"라는 시각적 이미지와 "are told"의 수동태가 소비자의 무력감을 전달한다. "with no clear date"가 불확실성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Retailers who depend on that warehouse
that the item is out of stock
with no clear date for restocking

pronunciationNotes: retailers: /ˈriːteɪlərz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "리테일러즈"에 가깝다.
restocking: /riːˈstɑːkɪŋ/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. re-가 "다시"를 뜻하는 접두사이다.

sentences.original: What began as a weather event at a single port has now affected manufacturers, shippers, warehouses, stores, and millions of consumers.
sentences.translation: 단일 항구에서의 기상 현상으로 시작된 것이 이제 제조업체, 운송업체, 창고, 매장, 그리고 수백만 소비자에게 영향을 미쳤다.

grammarElements.text: What began as a weather event at a single port
grammarElements.label: 주어 (What 명사절)

grammarElements.text: manufacturers, shippers, warehouses, stores, and millions of consumers
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (나열)

vocabulary.example: Manufacturers were affected by the delay.
Shippers faced rising costs during the crisis.
Millions of consumers were affected.

expressions.usage: 작은 시작이 큰 결과로 이어졌음을 대비시키는 표현
현재완료로 과거에 시작된 영향이 현재까지 이어짐을 나타낸다

patterns.pattern: What began as [small cause] has now affected [list of affected parties]
patterns.explanation: 작은 원인이 광범위한 결과로 이어진 상황을 요약하는 패턴. 연쇄 효과 서사의 결론에 쓴다.
examples: What began as a software glitch has now affected banks, airlines, hospitals, and millions of users.
What began as a small leak has now affected the basement, the first floor, the electrical system, and the entire building.

drillQuestions.question: _____ began as a weather event has now affected millions.
The event has now _____ manufacturers, shippers, and consumers.
"What began as" introduces a contrast between a small _____ and a large _____.
"Has now affected" uses the present _____ tense.

sentences.notes: 연쇄 효과 시나리오의 총결산. What 명사절(What began as ~)이 주어로 쓰여 작은 원인과 큰 결과의 대비를 극대화한다. 다섯 주체를 나열해 영향의 광범위함을 시각적으로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: What began as a weather event at a single port
koreanAlignment.ko: 단일 항구에서의 기상 현상으로 시작된 것이

pronunciationNotes: manufacturers: /ˌmænjuˈfæktʃərərz/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "매뉴팩처러즈"에 가깝다. 긴 단어이므로 천천히 연습한다.
consumers: /kənˈsuːmərz/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨수머즈"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: During the global disruptions of the early 2020s, the world saw this pattern play out on a massive scale.
sentences.translation: 2020년대 초반의 글로벌 혼란 동안, 세계는 이 패턴이 대규모로 전개되는 것을 목격했다.

grammarElements.text: During the global disruptions of the early 2020s
grammarElements.label: 시간 전치사구

grammarElements.text: play out on a massive scale
grammarElements.label: 목적격 보어 (지각동사 + 원형부정사)

vocabulary.example: The disruptions lasted for months.
This pattern repeats throughout history.
The project required a massive investment.

expressions.usage: 상황이나 사건이 실제로 진행되는 과정을 묘사할 때 쓴다
규모가 매우 크다는 것을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: During [time period], the world saw [noun] play out on a [adjective] scale
patterns.explanation: 특정 시기를 배경으로 제시하고, 지각동사 saw + 원형부정사로 세계가 목격한 현상을 서술하는 패턴.
examples: During the financial crisis of 2008, the world saw this pattern play out on a devastating scale.
During the pandemic, the world saw remote work play out on an unprecedented scale.

drillQuestions.question: During the early 2020s, the world _____ this pattern play out.
This pattern played out on a _____ scale.
"Play out" means to _____.
"Saw this pattern play out" uses a perception verb with a _____ infinitive.

sentences.notes: 가상 시나리오에서 실제 역사적 사례로 전환하는 핵심 문장. 지각동사 saw + 원형부정사(play out)가 세계가 직접 목격한 경험을 전달한다. "on a massive scale"이 가상 예시와의 규모 차이를 부각한다.

koreanAlignment.en: During the global disruptions of the early 2020s

pronunciationNotes: disruptions: /dɪsˈrʌpʃənz/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "디스럽션즈"에 가깝다.
massive: /ˈmæsɪv/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "매시브"에 가깝다. v를 확실히 발음한다.

sentences.original: Shipping containers piled up at ports, truck drivers were in short supply, and everyday items like paper towels and computer chips became difficult to find.
sentences.translation: 선적 컨테이너가 항구에 쌓이고, 트럭 운전사가 부족했으며, 화장지와 컴퓨터 칩 같은 일상용품을 구하기 어려워졌다.

grammarElements.text: Shipping containers piled up at ports
grammarElements.label: 병렬절 1

grammarElements.text: truck drivers were in short supply
grammarElements.label: 병렬절 2

grammarElements.text: and everyday items like paper towels and computer chips became difficult to find
grammarElements.label: 병렬절 3

vocabulary.example: The work piled up during the holiday.
Fresh vegetables were in short supply.
Computer chips are essential for modern electronics.

expressions.usage: 물건이 처리되지 못하고 한 곳에 누적되는 상황을 나타낸다
수요에 비해 공급이 충분하지 않은 상태를 나타내는 관용 표현

patterns.pattern: [Noun A] piled up at [place], [Noun B] were in short supply, and [Noun C] became difficult to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 세 가지 병렬 절로 혼란의 다양한 측면을 나열하는 패턴. 삼중 구조로 위기의 전방위적 영향을 보여준다.
examples: Orders piled up at warehouses, delivery drivers were in short supply, and basic goods became difficult to obtain.
Patients piled up at hospitals, nurses were in short supply, and routine surgeries became difficult to schedule.

drillQuestions.question: Shipping containers _____ up at ports.
Truck drivers were in _____ supply.
"Everyday items" means things people use _____.
Paper towels and computer chips became difficult to _____.

sentences.notes: 삼중 나열(piled up / were in short supply / became difficult to find)로 공급망 붕괴의 다차원적 영향을 보여준다. "paper towels and computer chips"라는 이질적 조합이 영향의 범위를 효과적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Shipping containers piled up at ports
truck drivers were in short supply
and everyday items like paper towels and computer chips

pronunciationNotes: containers: /kənˈteɪnərz/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨테이너즈"에 가깝다.
towels: /ˈtaʊəlz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "타월즈"에 가깝다. "타올"이 아니다.

sentences.original: Companies responded by rethinking their strategies: some moved production closer to their main markets, while others began keeping larger inventories as a safety buffer.
sentences.translation: 기업들은 전략을 재고함으로써 대응했다: 일부는 생산지를 주요 시장에 가깝게 옮겼고, 다른 일부는 안전 완충 장치로 더 큰 재고를 유지하기 시작했다.

grammarElements.text: some moved production closer to their main markets
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 병렬절 1 (some)

grammarElements.text: while others began keeping larger inventories as a safety buffer
grammarElements.label: 병렬절 2 (while others)

vocabulary.example: The company changed its strategies after the crisis.
Companies began keeping larger inventories.
We need a safety buffer in case of delays.

expressions.usage: by + 동명사로 대응 방식을 설명할 때 쓰는 표현
같은 그룹 내에서 서로 다른 대응을 대비시키는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] responded by [gerund]: some [action A], while others [action B]
patterns.explanation: 전체 집단의 대응을 소개한 뒤, 콜론으로 구체적 대응을 "some ~ while others ~"로 나누어 설명하는 패턴.
examples: Schools responded by adjusting their programs: some added online classes, while others extended the school year.
Governments responded by changing regulations: some tightened import rules, while others offered subsidies to local producers.

drillQuestions.question: Companies responded _____ rethinking their strategies.
Some companies moved production closer to their main _____.
"While others" introduces a _____ response.
Larger inventories serve as a safety _____.

sentences.notes: "responded by + 동명사"로 대응 방식을 소개하고, "some ~ while others ~"로 두 가지 전략을 대비시킨다. "safety buffer"는 공급망 관리의 핵심 개념(안전 재고)을 일반 독자에게 풀어 설명한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Companies responded by rethinking their strategies
some moved production closer to their main markets
koreanAlignment.ko: 일부는 생산지를 주요 시장에 가깝게 옮겼고

koreanAlignment.en: while others began keeping larger inventories
koreanAlignment.ko: 다른 일부는 더 큰 재고를 유지하기 시작했다

pronunciationNotes: strategies: /ˈstrætədʒiz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. str을 빠르게 발음. "스트래터지즈"에 가깝다.
inventories: /ˈɪnvəntɔːriz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "인번토리즈"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Governments also stepped in, investing in port upgrades and encouraging the development of alternative transportation routes.
sentences.translation: 정부도 나서서, 항구 개선에 투자하고 대체 운송 경로의 개발을 장려했다.

grammarElements.text: and encouraging the development of alternative transportation routes
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 2 (병렬)

vocabulary.example: The government stepped in to help.
Port upgrades were funded by the government.
We need alternative transportation routes.

expressions.usage: 외부 주체가 문제 해결을 위해 능동적으로 관여하는 것을 나타낸다
자금이나 노력을 특정 분야에 집중한다는 의미

patterns.pattern: [Subject] also stepped in, [present participle A] and [present participle B]
patterns.explanation: 추가적인 행위자가 개입한 것을 소개하며, 분사구문 두 개로 구체적 행동을 병렬 설명하는 패턴.
examples: Parents also stepped in, organizing fundraisers and volunteering at the school.
Nonprofits also stepped in, distributing food supplies and providing shelter for displaced families.

drillQuestions.question: Governments also _____ in to address the crisis.
They invested in port _____.
"Alternative transportation routes" means _____ ways to move goods.
"Investing" and "encouraging" are _____ participles used as modifiers.

sentences.notes: "also stepped in"으로 기업 대응에 이어 정부 대응을 추가한다. 분사구문(investing ~, encouraging ~)이 주절 뒤에 와서 구체적 행동을 효율적으로 나열한다.
pronunciationNotes: governments: /ˈɡʌvərnmənts/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "거번먼츠"에 가깝다. 가운데 n은 약하게 발음된다.
alternative: /ɔːlˈtɜːrnətɪv/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "올터너티브"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: These changes have made supply chains somewhat more resilient, but no system that spans the entire globe can be made fully immune to disruption.
sentences.translation: 이러한 변화는 공급망을 다소 더 탄력적으로 만들었지만, 전 세계에 걸친 어떤 시스템도 차질에 완전히 면역이 될 수는 없다.

grammarElements.text: no system that spans the entire globe
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절 (no + 명사)

grammarElements.text: can be made fully immune to disruption
grammarElements.label: 수동태 (can be made + 형용사)

vocabulary.example: The new system is more resilient to shocks.
The network spans three continents.
No company is immune to market changes.
The disruption affected the entire industry.

expressions.usage: somewhat로 변화의 정도를 조심스럽게 한정하는 표현
어떤 영향으로부터 완벽히 자유로울 수 없다는 의미로 쓴다

patterns.pattern: [Changes] have made [noun] somewhat more [adjective], but no [noun] that [description] can be made fully [adjective]
patterns.explanation: 개선을 인정하되(somewhat more ~), but과 no + 명사 구조로 근본적 한계를 지적하는 패턴. 균형 잡힌 결론에 쓴다.
examples: These reforms have made hospitals somewhat more efficient, but no healthcare system that serves millions can be made fully error-free.
New regulations have made banks somewhat more stable, but no financial system that connects globally can be made fully crash-proof.

drillQuestions.question: These changes have made supply chains somewhat more _____.
No system that spans the entire globe can be made fully _____ to disruption.
"Somewhat" means _____.
"No system ... can be made" uses a _____ construction with a negative subject.

sentences.notes: "somewhat more resilient"로 개선을 조심스럽게 인정한 뒤, "but no system ~ can be made fully immune"로 근본적 한계를 제시한다. "no + 명사 + 관계절 + 수동태" 구조가 논리적 불가능성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: These changes have made supply chains
but no system that spans the entire globe
can be made fully immune to disruption
koreanAlignment.ko: 차질에 완전히 면역이 될 수는 없다

pronunciationNotes: resilient: /rɪˈzɪliənt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "리질리언트"에 가깝다. s가 /z/로 발음된다.
immune: /ɪˈmjuːn/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이뮨"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The lesson is clear — the modern economy is a connected web, and what happens at one node is eventually felt everywhere else.
sentences.translation: 교훈은 분명하다 — 현대 경제는 연결된 거미줄이며, 한 마디에서 일어나는 일은 결국 다른 모든 곳에서 느껴진다.

grammarElements.text: the modern economy is a connected web
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 설명절 1

vocabulary.example: The lesson from this crisis is clear.
The global economy is deeply connected.
Each node in the network plays a role.

expressions.usage: 글의 핵심 메시지를 명시적으로 선언할 때 쓰는 결론 표현

expressions.phrase: what happens at one node is eventually felt everywhere else
expressions.meaning: 한 지점에서 일어나는 일이 결국 모든 곳에서 느껴진다
expressions.usage: what 명사절과 수동태로 상호 연결성의 불가피한 결과를 설명하는 표현

patterns.pattern: The lesson is clear — [metaphor], and what happens at [one place] is eventually felt [everywhere]
patterns.explanation: 결론부에서 교훈을 명시하고, 대시(—) 뒤에 은유와 What 명사절로 핵심 원리를 압축하는 패턴.
examples: The lesson is clear — a team is a chain, and what happens to one member is eventually felt by everyone else.
The lesson is clear — an ecosystem is a balance, and what happens to one species is eventually felt across the food web.

drillQuestions.question: The modern economy is a connected _____.
What happens at one node is _____ felt everywhere else.
"Is eventually felt" is in the _____ voice.
A "node" in this context means a _____ in the network.

sentences.notes: 글 전체의 결론. "The lesson is clear"로 명시적 마무리를 선언하고, "connected web"이라는 은유가 공급망의 상호의존성을 한 이미지로 응축한다. "what happens at one node is eventually felt everywhere else"의 수동태 What절이 글의 핵심 주제(연쇄 효과)를 반복한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the modern economy is a connected web
and what happens at one node

pronunciationNotes: economy: /ɪˈkɑːnəmi/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이카너미"에 가깝다.
eventually: /ɪˈventʃuəli/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이벤추얼리"에 가깝다.
